Package org.solenopsis.soap.metadata

Class OrchestrationContext

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.Metadata
org.solenopsis.soap.metadata.OrchestrationContext
public class OrchestrationContext extends Metadata

Java class for OrchestrationContext complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="OrchestrationContext">
   <complexContent>
     <extension base="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}Metadata">
       <sequence>
         <element name="datasets" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}OrchestrationContextDataset" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="description" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="events" type="{http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata}OrchestrationContextEvent" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="imageFile" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="imageScale" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}int"/>
         <element name="masterLabel" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="runtimeType" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="salesforceObject" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="salesforceObjectPrimaryKey" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string" minOccurs="0"/>
       </sequence>
     </extension>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

  • Method Details

    • getDatasets

      public List<OrchestrationContextDataset> getDatasets()
      Gets the value of the datasets property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the datasets property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getDatasets().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list OrchestrationContextDataset

      Returns:
      The value of the datasets property.

    • getDescription

      public String getDescription()
      Gets the value of the description property.
      Returns:
      possible object is String

    • setDescription

      public void setDescription(String value)
      Sets the value of the description property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is String

    • getEvents

      public List<OrchestrationContextEvent> getEvents()
      Gets the value of the events property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the events property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getEvents().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list OrchestrationContextEvent

      Returns:
      The value of the events property.
